article_title: Space: The Misguided Promise of Privatization
news_headline: SpaceX Raises $75 Billion in Record-Breaking IPO, Valued at $1.8 Trillion
post_text: SpaceX raised $75 billion in its IPO on June 12, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each and landing a $1.8 trillion valuation, more than double Saudi Aramco's $29.4 billion listing in 2019, and the largest public offering in history. The proceeds are earmarked largely for AI computing infrastructure and Starlink satellite expansion, which tells you this is less a space company IPO than a bet on whoever controls the next generation of compute and connectivity. Elon Musk retains over 82% voting control. The public now holds the equity; he holds the company. WBR's piece on the misguided promise of space privatization asked whether private space creates the value it claims, or whether it monetizes a foundation built by governments and taxpayers. At $1.8 trillion, that question has never been more worth revisiting. Read it on the WBR website. #BusinessAnalysis #SpaceX
final_body:
SpaceX raised $75 billion in its IPO on June 12, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each and landing a $1.8 trillion valuation, more than double Saudi Aramco's $29.4 billion listing in 2019, and the largest public offering in history. The proceeds are earmarked largely for AI computing infrastructure and Starlink satellite expansion, which tells you this is less a space company IPO than a bet on whoever controls the next generation of compute and connectivity. Elon Musk retains over 82% voting control. The public now holds the equity; he holds the company. WBR's piece on the misguided promise of space privatization asked whether private space creates the value it claims, or whether it monetizes a foundation built by governments and taxpayers. At $1.8 trillion, that question has never been more worth revisiting. Read it on the WBR website. #BusinessAnalysis #SpaceX
